title: rosa canina subvar. flexibilis
intro: Rosa canina subvar. flexibilis, also known as the flexible dog rose or Rosa canina, is a subspecies of the dog rose plant, characterized by its flexible and thornless branches. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its high content of vitamin C and flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. flexibilis include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Rosa canina subvar. flexibilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its dried flowers and hips can be used to make jams, jellies, and other edible products.
